title: Direct messages (1:1 whisper conversations)
description:
Expose the existing Whisper/WhisperDeliver relay primitives as a user-facing DM feature. The relay routing is already implemented; the work is one relay fix, MLS 2-party group setup, and client conversation management.

## Relay fix (one line)
Whispers to offline recipients are currently silently dropped. ws.rs handles Whisper with deliver_live() only — no enqueue() call. Fix: add store.enqueue(to, WhisperDeliver{from, payload}) when deliver_live() finds the recipient offline (same pattern as Broadcast offline queueing).

## MLS 2-party setup
Before the first DM can be sent, sender and recipient need a shared MLS group:
1. Sender fetches recipient KeyPackage via GetKeyPackage (already works)
2. Sender creates 2-party MLS group, generates Commit + Welcome
3. Sender sends Welcome to recipient via Whisper (already works — this is the existing MLS welcome flow)
4. Both sides now share an MLS epoch; DMs are encrypted ClearMessage::Chat payloads inside Whisper

Phase 1 (pre-MLS): DMs are plaintext ClearMessage::Chat inside Whisper, same as current broadcast. Upgrade to MLS-encrypted automatically once nie-t4t lands.

## Client changes
- New ClearMessage variant or reuse ClearMessage::Chat — the payload is identical to a broadcast chat message, just routed point-to-point via Whisper instead of Broadcast
- Conversation model: maintain a per-peer message history (separate from the room log)
- CLI: /dm <handle> <text> — send a DM; or /dm <handle> to open a DM session
- TUI: separate DM thread pane or tab per peer
- Notifications: distinguish DM delivery from room messages in all clients

## What the relay does NOT need
- No new DB tables (whispers use existing offline queue)
- No new message types (Whisper/WhisperDeliver already exist)
- No routing changes (deliver_live already handles it)

## Dependencies
- nie-hlns (JSON-RPC protocol) — DM client implements Whisper over the updated protocol
